candidates.direction_name: Transplant low-register half-panel staging into the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot
ranking_notes: This family moved to rank 1 because the rich NCU handoff makes registers/thread the dominant hotspot instead of export bookkeeping.
It is ahead of the PTX-local retune because it is the only active family with explicit historical evidence of breaking the low-warp plateau.
The risk is real, but the queue needs at least one forward candidate that attacks the primary bottleneck directly.
candidates.hypothesis: The richer NCU summary says the live queue should prioritize the only active family that directly attacks 200 registers per thread and the 16.63% active-warps ceiling. The correctness-safe 256x128 pivot should keep its current writer ownership, but transplant the compact staging and register-budget ideas that historically moved occupancy in the right direction.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: occupancy_latency_hiding_issue with tensor_core_underutilization driven by register pressure and oversized live state
candidates.risk: High: this is the best hotspot-aligned family, but it touches a broader geometry and could reintroduce correctness or shared-budget regressions if the old ownership split leaks back in.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if registers per thread stay at or above 200, if active warps do not improve, or if writer ownership changes trigger correctness failures.

expected_local_changes: Compact the 256x128 live fragment footprint without reviving the broken half-panel writer split.
Reduce accumulator or staging lifetime before touching export-side arithmetic.
Keep the existing shared-memory budget discipline visible in the 256x128 path.

candidates.direction_name: Trim live state inside the active 128x128 PTX control path before more epilogue work
ranking_notes: This became the best PTX-local family after the richer NCU summary showed that registers and occupancy matter more than export address arithmetic.
It stays below dir_01 because the 256x128 transplant attacks the primary hotspot more directly.
It stays above the barrier-only families because a register drop on the accepted surface can improve both latency hiding and barrier sensitivity at once.
candidates.hypothesis: The richer NCU summary weakens the export-address thesis and strengthens a different PTX-local read: the accepted 128x128 PTX microkernel is carrying too much live state through its steady-state control path. Retuning fragment lifetime, grouped-row control state, or export handoff sequencing on the accepted surface should be tried before another epilogue-only cleanup.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: occupancy_latency_hiding_issue on the accepted PTX hot-band path, with a smaller synchronization_barrier_issue component
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:2056 (PTX export handoff on the accepted surface)
candidates.risk: Medium: this stays on the fastest correctness-proven branch, but lifetime and handoff changes can still flatten gains if they only shuffle stalls around without reducing registers.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if registers do not fall, if active warps stay flat, or if tensor activity drops below the current 48.4% level.

candidates.direction_name: Collapse PTX wait-group and sync cadence without growing the shared-memory footprint
ranking_notes: This barrier family stays in the top three because the richer NCU summary still shows a real CTA handoff cost and the previous delta slightly regressed barrier.
It is below dir_02 because barrier is secondary to the register and occupancy wall.
It is ahead of the export families because the structured summary no longer shows an export-dominant hotspot.
candidates.hypothesis: Barrier is not the dominant issue, but the richer NCU handoff still flags the wait/sync seam as the best secondary hotspot. The next barrier-focused family should keep the current 22,016 B shared footprint and trim the PTX wait-group plus __syncthreads cadence instead of repeating any x32 shared-memory expansion.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: synchronization_barrier_issue with smaller occupancy side-effects on the PTX 128x128 microkernel
candidates.risk: Medium-high: the barrier seam is fragile and can break correctness or pipeline overlap if the retime outruns cp.async visibility.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if barrier stalls fail to improve, if tensor activity regresses, or if the retime introduces correctness instability.
